heading: Official badges, identification cards, other insignia
label: 701 Official badges, identification cards, other insignia
text: Whoever manufactures, sells, or possesses any badge, identification card, or other insignia, of the design prescribed by the head of any department or agency of the United States for use by any officer or employee thereof, or any colorable imitation thereof, or photographs, prints, or in any other manner makes or executes any engraving, photograph, print, or impression in the likeness of any such badge, identification card, or other insignia, or any colorable imitation thereof, except as authorized under regulations made pursuant to law, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than six months, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 76a, 76b (, §§ 1, 2, ; , ).June 29, 1932, ch. 30647 Stat. 342May 22, 1939, ch. 14153 Stat. 752Sections were consolidated.The term “department or agency” was substituted for “department or independent office” in two places to embrace all properly constituted agencies as defined in and to eliminate any possible ambiguity as to scope of section.section 6 of this titleMinor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $250”.Pub. L. 103–322
